name: cp-helper
description: Run competitive programming samples against your C++ solution in Visual Studio Code (official Microsoft build only).

cp-helper.compileCommand.description: C++ compile line; empty skips compile (use only if you pre-build the binary that runCommand runs). Placeholders: {{file}}, {{dir}} (source dir), {{out}} (temp binary).

cp-helper.runCommand.description: Shell command run with each test’s stdin; default runs the compiled C++ binary at {{out}}. For a wrapper script, use invokeViaLoginShell instead of embedding bash -lc here.

cp-helper.invokeViaLoginShell.description: If true, compile and run commands are executed via loginShellInvoke (loads ~/.bashrc etc.) so a shell alias/function named `run` works.

cp-helper.loginShellInvoke.description: Prefix used when invokeViaLoginShell is true; the full command is passed as a single quoted argument (e.g. zsh -il -c for zsh login).

cp-helper.runTimeoutMs.description: Compile and run are killed after this many ms. The whole process tree is stopped (not only the shell), so TLE applies to the real program.

cp-helper.floatAbsEpsilon.description: Maximum allowed absolute error when comparing numeric output tokens to expected (e.g. 1e-9 or 1e-12 per problem statement). Invalid values fall back to 1e-9.

cp-helper.enableLocalImportServer.description: Listen on 127.0.0.1 so the OJ Sync browser extension can POST samples JSON without a vscode:// URL (avoids Chrome’s “Open URL handler?” prompt).

cp-helper.localImportPort.description: Port for the local import HTTP server (POST /import, body = samples JSON array). Change if the port is already in use (e.g. another VS Code window).

cp-helper.instantRunAllOnLocalImport.description: When samples arrive via local POST /import (e.g. OJ Sync): after import, start Run all only if the payload normalizes to a single problem group (e.g. one AtCoder task or one Codeforces problem). Multi-group imports (e.g. full contest dump) do not auto-run.

cp-helper.trimOutput.description: Trim trailing whitespace when comparing to expected output.

cp-helper.workingDirectory.description: Cwd for compile/run (empty = source file directory).

cp-helper.showOutputOnRun.description: Open the Output panel (channel CP Helper) when running tests; uses preserveFocus so the editor stays active. Off by default — use command “CP Helper: Show Output Log” when you want the log.

cp-helper.floatRelEpsilon.description: Maximum allowed relative error when comparing numeric tokens (e.g. 1e-6 = 0.0001%). Applied only when absolute error check fails. 0 disables relative comparison (default).

cp-helper.checkerCommand.description: Optional custom checker command run after WA (for problems with multiple correct answers). Placeholders: {{file}}, {{out}} (binary), {{input}} (temp stdin file), {{expected}} (temp expected file), {{actual}} (temp actual output file). Exit 0 = AC, non-zero = WA. Example: ./checker {{input}} {{expected}} {{actual}}

cp-helper.stressGeneratorCommand.description: Shell command for stress testing whose stdout is the raw test input for each iteration. Example: python3 gen.py

cp-helper.stressReferenceCommand.description: Optional reference (brute-force) solution for stress testing. Its stdout is compared against cp-helper.runCommand output. If empty, only RE and TLE are detected.

cp-helper.stressMaxIterations.description: Maximum number of stress-test iterations before stopping (default 100).

scripts.vsix:bump: npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && npm run vsix
scripts.vsix:local:run: vsce package -o cp-helper.vsix && code --install-extension cp-helper.vsix --force --profile "Problem Solving [ C++ ]"
